notes: Students should complete core programming and math requirements before upper-level systems and AI electives.
advising_tips: Plan early for COSC490 and make sure foundational programming plus math courses are completed before heavy 300 and 400 level terms.

notes: Students usually balance business core requirements with information systems and analytics courses.
advising_tips: Prioritize database analytics and business process courses early if you want internships in consulting or enterprise systems.

notes: Pre-health students should coordinate major requirements with professional school prerequisites.
advising_tips: Meet with a department advisor each semester if you are preparing for medical dental pharmacy or research pathways.

required_courses: PSYC101; PSYC210; PSYC301; PSYC302; STAT302; BIOL101; ENGL101; ENGL102
notes: Psychology students need strong research and writing preparation for internships and graduate study.
advising_tips: Take statistics and research methods on time so you are ready for upper-level lab and practicum opportunities.

required_courses: ENGE101; ENGE102; EEGR203; EEGR205; EEGR301; EEGR317; MATH141; MATH241; PHYS101; PHYS102
notes: Engineering students should sequence math physics and circuits carefully because later courses build directly on them.
advising_tips: Avoid delaying calculus physics or circuits because that can slow progress toward design-intensive upper-level coursework.
